<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/src/components/curves/SaturationCurve.tsx`

```text
     1→import { useMemo } from "react";
     2→import { getSaturation } from "../../lib/palette";
     3→import { usePaletteStore } from "../../store/paletteStore";
     4→import type { ColorEntry } from "../../types/palette";
     5→
     6→interface SaturationCurveProps {
     7→  entries: ColorEntry[];
     8→}
     9→
    10→const W = 480;
    11→const H = 240;
    12→const PAD = { top: 24, right: 24, bottom: 36, left: 48 };
    13→const PLOT_W = W - PAD.left - PAD.right;
    14→const PLOT_H = H - PAD.top - PAD.bottom;
    15→
    16→export function SaturationCurve({ entries }: SaturationCurveProps) {
    17→  const sMod = usePaletteStore((s) => s.sMod);
    18→  const saturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.saturation);
    19→
    20→  const { curvePath, yMin, yMax, effectiveDots, gridLines } = useMemo(() => {
    21→    // Sample the curve at every integer lightness
    22→    const points: { x: number; y: number }[] = [];
    23→    for (let l = 0; l <= 100; l++) {
    24→      points.push({ x: l, y: getSaturation(l, sMod) });
    25→    }
    26→
    27→    const allY = points.map((p) => p.y);
    28→    const yMin = Math.min(...allY, 0);
    29→    const yMax = Math.max(...allY) * 1.1;
    30→
    31→    const toSvgX = (l: number) => PAD.left + (l / 100) * PLOT_W;
    32→    const toSvgY = (v: number) => PAD.top + (1 - (v - yMin) / (yMax - yMin)) * PLOT_H;
    33→
    34→    // Build SVG path for the multiplier curve
    35→    const pathParts = points.map((p, i) => {
    36→      const cmd = i === 0 ? "M" : "L";
    37→      return `${cmd}${toSvgX(p.x).toFixed(2)},${toSvgY(p.y).toFixed(2)}`;
    38→    });
    39→
    40→    // Dots for each palette entry showing effective saturation
    41→    const effectiveDots = entries.map((e) => {
    42→      const sK = getSaturation(e.lightness, sMod);
    43→      const effective = saturation * sK;
    44→      return {
    45→        cx: toSvgX(e.lightness),
    46→        cy: toSvgY(sK),
    47→        effective: Math.round(effective * 100),
    48→        lightness: e.lightness,
    49→        hex: e.hex,
    50→      };
    51→    });
    52→
    53→    // Horizontal grid lines
    54→    const gridCount = 4;
    55→    const gridLines = Array.from({ length: gridCount + 1 }, (_, i) => {
    56→      const v = yMin + (i / gridCount) * (yMax - yMin);
    57→      return {
    58→        y: toSvgY(v),
    59→        label: v.toFixed(2),
    60→      };
    61→    });
    62→
    63→    return { curvePath: pathParts.join(""), yMin, yMax, effectiveDots, gridLines };
    64→  }, [sMod, saturation, entries]);
    65→
    66→  // Vertical grid lines at lightness landmarks
    67→  const xGridValues = [0, 25, 50, 75, 100];
    68→  const toSvgX = (l: number) => PAD.left + (l / 100) * PLOT_W;
    69→
    70→  return (
    71→    <div className="mx-4 rounded-xl border border-white/[0.06] bg-white/[0.02] overflow-hidden">
    72→      <div className="px-4 py-3 border-b border-white/[0.06] flex items-center justify-between">
    73→        <div>
    74→          <h3 className="text-[13px] font-semibold text-white/70 tracking-wide">
    75→            Saturation Curve
    76→          </h3>
    77→          <p className="text-[11px] text-white/30 mt-0.5">
    78→            Multiplier across lightness &middot; dots show palette steps
    79→          </p>
    80→        </div>
    81→        <div className="flex gap-4 text-[11px] font-mono text-white/40">
    82→          <span>
    83→            <span className="inline-block w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-amber-400 mr-1 align-middle" />
    84→            multiplier
    85→          </span>
    86→          <span>
    87→            <span className="inline-block w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-white/50 mr-1 align-middle" />
    88→            palette step
    89→          </span>
    90→        </div>
    91→      </div>
    92→      <svg
    93→        viewBox={`0 0 ${W} ${H}`}
    94→        className="w-full h-auto"
    95→        role="img"
    96→        aria-label="Saturation curve showing how the saturation multiplier varies across lightness values"
    97→      >
    98→        {/* Grid lines - horizontal */}
    99→        {gridLines.map((g) => (
   100→          <g key={g.label}>
   101→            <line
   102→              x1={PAD.left}
   103→              y1={g.y}
   104→              x2={W - PAD.right}
   105→              y2={g.y}
   106→              stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.06)"
   107→              strokeWidth={1}
   108→            />
   109→            <text
   110→              x={PAD.left - 8}
   111→              y={g.y + 3}
   112→              textAnchor="end"
   113→              className="text-[9px] fill-white/25 font-mono"
   114→            >
   115→              {g.label}
   116→            </text>
   117→          </g>
   118→        ))}
   119→
   120→        {/* Grid lines - vertical */}
   121→        {xGridValues.map((l) => (
   122→          <g key={l}>
   123→            <line
   124→              x1={toSvgX(l)}
   125→              y1={PAD.top}
   126→              x2={toSvgX(l)}
   127→              y2={H - PAD.bottom}
   128→              stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.06)"
   129→              strokeWidth={1}
   130→            />
   131→            <text
   132→              x={toSvgX(l)}
   133→              y={H - PAD.bottom + 16}
   134→              textAnchor="middle"
   135→              className="text-[9px] fill-white/25 font-mono"
   136→            >
   137→              {l}
   138→            </text>
   139→          </g>
   140→        ))}
   141→
   142→        {/* Axis labels */}
   143→        <text
   144→          x={PAD.left + PLOT_W / 2}
   145→          y={H - 4}
   146→          textAnchor="middle"
   147→          className="text-[10px] fill-white/30 font-sans"
   148→        >
   149→          Lightness
   150→        </text>
   151→        <text
   152→          x={12}
   153→          y={PAD.top + PLOT_H / 2}
   154→          textAnchor="middle"
   155→          className="text-[10px] fill-white/30 font-sans"
   156→          transform={`rotate(-90, 12, ${PAD.top + PLOT_H / 2})`}
   157→        >
   158→          Multiplier
   159→        </text>
   160→
   161→        {/* 1.0 baseline */}
   162→        <line
   163→          x1={PAD.left}
   164→          y1={PAD.top + (1 - (1 - yMin) / (yMax - yMin)) * PLOT_H}
   165→          x2={W - PAD.right}
   166→          y2={PAD.top + (1 - (1 - yMin) / (yMax - yMin)) * PLOT_H}
   167→          stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.12)"
   168→          strokeWidth={1}
   169→          strokeDasharray="4 3"
   170→        />
   171→
   172→        {/* Saturation curve */}
   173→        <path
   174→          d={curvePath}
   175→          fill="none"
   176→          stroke="hsl(38 90% 55%)"
   177→          strokeWidth={2}
   178→          strokeLinecap="round"
   179→          strokeLinejoin="round"
   180→        />
   181→
   182→        {/* Palette entry dots */}
   183→        {effectiveDots.map((d) => (
   184→          <circle
   185→            key={d.lightness}
   186→            cx={d.cx}
   187→            cy={d.cy}
   188→            r={3}
   189→            fill={d.hex}
   190→            stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.4)"
   191→            strokeWidth={1}
   192→          />
   193→        ))}
   194→      </svg>
   195→    </div>
   196→  );
   197→}
   198→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-13 12:32:46Z)**_

The slider itself is linear (25–2500), but `sMod` has an inversely proportional effect on the curve — the formula divides by `sMod` (`(L-50)² / sMod`). So:

- Moving from 25→50 (a change of 25) dramatically flattens the curve
- Moving from 2000→2025 (same absolute change) is barely perceptible

The perceptual impact is concentrated at the low end. The original CodePen had the same range. It's linear in slider position but non-linear in visual effect.

Want me to remap it so the slider feels more even — e.g., use a quadratic or exponential mapping so small slider movements at the high end have more impact?

_**Agent - sidechain (2026-03-13 12:33:39Z)**_

but the adjust number is non linear. the rightmost position is -1%, the midpoint is -2%, the leftmost position is -100% whats up with that

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-13 12:33:46Z)**_



That's because the display formula is `-(50² / sMod)` — it's a reciprocal relationship. The slider is linear in `sMod` (25–2500), but the displayed percentage is `1/sMod`, so:

- sMod 25 → -100%
- sMod 1250 → -2%
- sMod 2500 → -1%

The values cluster near zero at the right end and spike at the left. It's confusing.

The simplest fix: make the slider linear in the displayed percentage and convert to `sMod` internally. That way the label moves evenly with the thumb. Want me to do that?

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-13 12:34:54Z)**_

The display value is `-(2500/sMod)`, which ranges from -1% (sMod=2500) to -100% (sMod=25). I'll make the slider linear in that percentage and derive `sMod` from it.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-13 12:34:58Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
import { usePaletteStore } from "../../store/paletteStore";
import { SliderBase } from "./SliderBase";

// The display value is -(50^2 / sMod) = -(2500 / sMod), range -1 to -100.
// Slider is linear in this percentage; we convert to/from sMod internally.
function pctToSMod(pct: number): number {
  return 2500 / -pct;
}

function sModToPct(sMod: number): number {
  return -2500 / sMod;
}

export function AdjustmentSlider() {
  const sMod = usePaletteStore((s) => s.sMod);
  const setSMod = usePaletteStore((s) => s.setSMod);

  const pct = Math.round(sModToPct(sMod));

  return (
    <SliderBase
      label="Adjustment"
      value={pct}
      min={-100}
      max={-1}
      step={1}
      onValueChange={(v) => setSMod(pctToSMod(v))}
      formatValue={(v) => `${v}%`}
    />
  );
}

```

_**Agent - sidechain (2026-03-13 12:37:00Z)**_

why is there no 0% adjustment?

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-13 12:37:07Z)**_



Because at 0% the formula would be `2500 / 0` = infinity — `sMod` would be infinite, which means the parabola is completely flat (multiplier = 1 everywhere, no curve adjustment at all). The slider can't represent that.

Options:
1. Cap it at -1% as-is (current behavior)
2. Add 0% as a special case that maps to a very large `sMod` (e.g. 100000), effectively flattening the curve
3. Rethink the range — maybe 0% to -100% where 0% means "no adjustment" and use a clamped large value

Option 3 feels most intuitive — 0% = no curve, -100% = maximum curve. Want me to go with that?

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/src/lib/palette.ts`

```text
     1→import chroma from "chroma-js";
     2→import type { ColorEntry, PaletteParams } from "../types/palette";
     3→
     4→/**
     5→ * Parabolic saturation curve centered at lightness 50%.
     6→ * See: https://www.desmos.com/calculator/02ufrfsuzy
     7→ */
     8→export function getSaturation(lightness: number, sMod: number): number {
     9→  const o = 50;
    10→  return 1 + (Math.pow(lightness - o, 2) / sMod - Math.pow(o, 2) / sMod) / 100;
    11→}
    12→
    13→export function generateNeutrals(params: PaletteParams): ColorEntry[] {
    14→  const { hue, saturation, sMod } = params;
    15→  const min = 0.03;
    16→  const max = 1.01;
    17→  const distance = 0.01;
    18→
    19→  const entries: ColorEntry[] = [];
    20→  let value = min;
    21→  let step = 0;
    22→
    23→  while (value < max) {
    24→    const i = Math.floor(value * 100);
    25→    const sK = getSaturation(i, sMod);
    26→    const color = chroma.hsl(hue, saturation * sK, value);
    27→
    28→    const [h, s, l] = color.hsl();
    29→    const hRound = Math.round(h) || 0;
    30→    const sRound = Math.round(Math.round((s + Number.EPSILON) * 100 * 100) / 100);
    31→    const lRound = Math.round(Math.round((l + Number.EPSILON) * 100 * 100) / 100);
    32→
    33→    entries.push({
    34→      label: `grey-${i}`,
    35→      lightness: i,
    36→      hsl: [h, s, l],
    37→      hslString: `${hRound}deg ${sRound}% ${lRound}%`,
    38→      hex: color.hex(),
    39→    });
    40→
    41→    step++;
    42→    value = step * distance + min;
    43→  }
    44→
    45→  return entries.reverse();
    46→}
    47→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
    14→export function generateNeutrals(params: PaletteParams): ColorEntry[] {
    15→  const { hue, saturation, sMod } = params;
    16→  const min = 0.03;
    17→  const max = 1.01;
    18→  const distance = 0.01;
    19→
    20→  const entries: ColorEntry[] = [];
    21→  let value = min;
    22→  let step = 0;
    23→
    24→  while (value < max) {
    25→    const i = Math.floor(value * 100);
    26→    const sK = getSaturation(i, sMod);
    27→    const color = chroma.hsl(hue, saturation * sK, value);
    28→
    29→    const [h, s, l] = color.hsl();
    30→    const hRound = Math.round(h) || 0;
    31→    const sRound = Math.round(Math.round((s + Number.EPSILON) * 100 * 100) / 100);
    32→    const lRound = Math.round(Math.round((l + Number.EPSILON) * 100 * 100) / 100);

    35→      label: `grey-${i}`,

    40→    });
    41→

    44→  }

    46→  return entries.reverse();
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
import { useMemo } from "react";
import { getSaturation } from "../../lib/palette";
import { usePaletteStore } from "../../store/paletteStore";
import type { ColorEntry } from "../../types/palette";

interface EffectiveSaturationProps {
  entries: ColorEntry[];
}

const W = 400;
const H = 400;
const PAD = { top: 24, right: 24, bottom: 36, left: 48 };
const PLOT_W = W - PAD.left - PAD.right;
const PLOT_H = H - PAD.top - PAD.bottom;

export function EffectiveSaturation({ entries }: EffectiveSaturationProps) {
  const sMod = usePaletteStore((s) => s.sMod);
  const saturation = usePaletteStore((s) => s.saturation);

  const { curvePath, baseLine, dots, gridLines } = useMemo(() => {
    // Sample effective saturation at every integer lightness
    const points: { x: number; y: number }[] = [];
    for (let l = 0; l <= 100; l++) {
      points.push({ x: l, y: saturation * getSaturation(l, sMod) });
    }

    const allY = points.map((p) => p.y);
    const yMax = Math.max(...allY, saturation) * 1.15;
    const yMin = 0;

    const toSvgX = (l: number) => PAD.left + (l / 100) * PLOT_W;
    const toSvgY = (v: number) => PAD.top + (1 - (v - yMin) / (yMax - yMin)) * PLOT_H;

    // Effective saturation curve path
    const pathParts = points.map((p, i) => {
      const cmd = i === 0 ? "M" : "L";
      return `${cmd}${toSvgX(p.x).toFixed(2)},${toSvgY(p.y).toFixed(2)}`;
    });

    // Flat baseline at base saturation
    const baseY = toSvgY(saturation);
    const baseLine = {
      y: baseY,
      label: `${Math.round(saturation * 100)}%`,
    };

    // Dots at palette entry positions
    const dots = entries.map((e) => {
      const sK = getSaturation(e.lightness, sMod);
      const effective = saturation * sK;
      return {
        cx: toSvgX(e.lightness),
        cy: toSvgY(effective),
        lightness: e.lightness,
        hex: e.hex,
      };
    });

    // Horizontal grid lines at round percentage values
    const step = yMax > 0.5 ? 0.1 : 0.05;
    const gridVals: number[] = [];
    for (let v = 0; v <= yMax; v += step) {
      gridVals.push(parseFloat(v.toFixed(2)));
    }
    const gridLines = gridVals.map((v) => ({
      y: toSvgY(v),
      label: `${Math.round(v * 100)}%`,
    }));

    return { curvePath: pathParts.join(""), baseLine, dots, gridLines };
  }, [sMod, saturation, entries]);

  const xGridValues = [0, 25, 50, 75, 100];
  const toSvgX = (l: number) => PAD.left + (l / 100) * PLOT_W;

  return (
    <div className="mx-4 rounded-xl border border-white/[0.06] bg-white/[0.02] overflow-hidden">
      <div className="px-4 py-3 border-b border-white/[0.06] flex items-center justify-between">
        <h3 className="text-[13px] font-semibold text-white/70 tracking-wide">
          Effective Saturation
        </h3>
        <div className="flex gap-4 text-[11px] font-mono text-white/40">
          <span>
            <span className="inline-block w-2 h-0.5 bg-white/25 mr-1 align-middle" />
            base
          </span>
          <span>
            <span className="inline-block w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-white/50 mr-1 align-middle" />
            palette step
          </span>
        </div>
      </div>
      <svg
        viewBox={`0 0 ${W} ${H}`}
        className="w-full h-auto"
        role="img"
        aria-label="Effective saturation distribution across lightness values"
      >
        {/* Grid lines - horizontal */}
        {gridLines.map((g) => (
          <g key={g.label}>
            <line
              x1={PAD.left}
              y1={g.y}
              x2={W - PAD.right}
              y2={g.y}
              stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.06)"
              strokeWidth={1}
            />
            <text
              x={PAD.left - 8}
              y={g.y + 3}
              textAnchor="end"
              className="text-[11px] fill-white/30 font-mono"
            >
              {g.label}
            </text>
          </g>
        ))}

        {/* Grid lines - vertical */}
        {xGridValues.map((l) => (
          <g key={l}>
            <line
              x1={toSvgX(l)}
              y1={PAD.top}
              x2={toSvgX(l)}
              y2={H - PAD.bottom}
              stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.06)"
              strokeWidth={1}
            />
            <text
              x={toSvgX(l)}
              y={H - PAD.bottom + 16}
              textAnchor="middle"
              className="text-[11px] fill-white/30 font-mono"
            >
              {l}
            </text>
          </g>
        ))}

        {/* Axis labels */}
        <text
          x={PAD.left + PLOT_W / 2}
          y={H - 4}
          textAnchor="middle"
          className="text-[10px] fill-white/30 font-sans"
        >
          Lightness
        </text>
        <text
          x={12}
          y={PAD.top + PLOT_H / 2}
          textAnchor="middle"
          className="text-[10px] fill-white/30 font-sans"
          transform={`rotate(-90, 12, ${PAD.top + PLOT_H / 2})`}
        >
          Saturation
        </text>

        {/* Base saturation line (flat / unadjusted) */}
        <line
          x1={PAD.left}
          y1={baseLine.y}
          x2={W - PAD.right}
          y2={baseLine.y}
          stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.2)"
          strokeWidth={1}
          strokeDasharray="4 3"
        />

        {/* Effective saturation curve */}
        <path
          d={curvePath}
          fill="none"
          stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.7)"
          strokeWidth={2}
          strokeLinecap="round"
          strokeLinejoin="round"
        />

        {/* Palette entry dots */}
        {dots.map((d) => (
          <circle
            key={d.lightness}
            cx={d.cx}
            cy={d.cy}
            r={3}
            fill={d.hex}
            stroke="rgba(255,255,255,0.4)"
            strokeWidth={1}
          />
        ))}
      </svg>
    </div>
  );
}
```

hm i'm wondering if "effective saturation" is clear. could be "computed saturation", "final saturation", thoughts?"

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-13 13:01:57Z)**_



"Effective" sounds like it's describing a quality rather than a result. I'd lean toward **Saturation Output** — it's the output of the formula, parallels "Saturation Adjustment" nicely (input → output), and is unambiguous.

"Final Saturation" works too but implies a sequence the user might not be thinking about. "Computed" feels too technical.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-13 13:12:49Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
import { useState } from "react";
import { ColorSwatch } from "./ColorSwatch";
import { useCopyToClipboard } from "../../hooks/useCopyToClipboard";
import type { ColorEntry } from "../../types/palette";

interface PaletteGridProps {
  entries: ColorEntry[];
}

export function PaletteGrid({ entries }: PaletteGridProps) {
  const { copiedLabel, copy } = useCopyToClipboard();
  const [showBorders, setShowBorders] = useState(true);

  return (
    <div>
      <div className="flex justify-end px-4 lg:pr-0 mb-1">
        <label className="flex items-center gap-1 cursor-pointer">
          <span className="text-[9px] font-mono text-white/20">borders</span>
          <button
            type="button"
            role="switch"
            aria-checked={showBorders}
            onClick={() => setShowBorders((v) => !v)}
            className="relative w-5 h-2.5 rounded-full transition-colors"
            style={{ backgroundColor: showBorders ? "rgba(255,255,255,0.25)" : "rgba(255,255,255,0.06)" }}
          >
            <span
              className="absolute top-px left-px w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-white/70 transition-transform"
              style={{ transform: showBorders ? "translateX(10px)" : "translateX(0)" }}
            />
          </button>
        </label>
      </div>
      <div
        className={`grid grid-cols-[repeat(auto-fill,minmax(180px,1fr))] ml-4 mr-4 lg:mr-0 rounded-xl overflow-hidden ${showBorders ? "gap-px bg-white/[0.04]" : "gap-0"}`}
      >
        {entries.map((entry) => (
          <ColorSwatch
            key={entry.label}
            entry={entry}
            isCopied={copiedLabel === entry.label}
            onCopy={copy}
          />
        ))}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-13 13:16:00Z)**_



You're right — "all" is misleading. It's really just however many the current step size produces (~98). The count should always be explicit. I'll replace "all" with the actual number and make it the default.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-13 13:16:01Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/src/components/palette/PaletteGrid.tsx`

```text
    10→const STEP_OPTIONS = [8, 10, 12, 16, 20, 24, 48, 0] as const;
    11→
    12→function sampleEntries(entries: ColorEntry[], count: number): ColorEntry[] {
    13→  if (count === 0 || count >= entries.length) return entries;
    14→  const step = (entries.length - 1) / (count - 1);
    15→  return Array.from({ length: count }, (_, i) =>
    16→    entries[Math.round(i * step)],
    17→  );
    18→}
    19→
    20→export function PaletteGrid({ entries }: PaletteGridProps) {
    21→  const { copiedLabel, copy } = useCopyToClipboard();
    22→  const [showBorders, setShowBorders] = useState(true);
    23→  const [showLabels, setShowLabels] = useState(true);
    24→  const [swatchCount, setSwatchCount] = useState<number>(0);
```

```tsx
import { useMemo, useState } from "react";
import { ColorSwatch } from "./ColorSwatch";
import { useCopyToClipboard } from "../../hooks/useCopyToClipboard";
import type { ColorEntry } from "../../types/palette";

interface PaletteGridProps {
  entries: ColorEntry[];
}

const STEP_OPTIONS = [8, 10, 12, 16, 20, 24, 48, 98];
const COL_OPTIONS = [4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 10, 12];

type Layout = "grid" | "row" | "column";

function sampleEntries(entries: ColorEntry[], count: number): ColorEntry[] {
  if (count >= entries.length) return entries;
  const step = (entries.length - 1) / (count - 1);
  return Array.from({ length: count }, (_, i) =>
    entries[Math.round(i * step)],
  );
}

// Trim entries so the last row is always full
function trimToFillGrid(entries: ColorEntry[], cols: number): ColorEntry[] {
  const remainder = entries.length % cols;
  if (remainder === 0) return entries;
  return entries.slice(0, entries.length - remainder);
}

// Reorder entries for column-major layout (read top→bottom, left→right)
// rendered in a row-major CSS grid
function columnMajorOrder(entries: ColorEntry[], cols: number): ColorEntry[] {
  const rows = Math.ceil(entries.length / cols);
  const reordered: ColorEntry[] = [];
  for (let r = 0; r < rows; r++) {
    for (let c = 0; c < cols; c++) {
      const idx = c * rows + r;
      if (idx < entries.length) reordered.push(entries[idx]);
    }
  }
  return reordered;
}

export function PaletteGrid({ entries }: PaletteGridProps) {
  const { copiedLabel, copy } = useCopyToClipboard();
  const [showBorders, setShowBorders] = useState(true);
  const [showLabels, setShowLabels] = useState(true);
  const [swatchCount, setSwatchCount] = useState<number>(98);
  const [cols, setCols] = useState<number>(7);
  const [layout, setLayout] = useState<Layout>("grid");

  const visibleEntries = useMemo(() => {
    const sampled = sampleEntries(entries, swatchCount);
    if (layout === "row" || layout === "column") return sampled;
    const trimmed = trimToFillGrid(sampled, cols);
    return trimmed;
  }, [entries, swatchCount, cols, layout]);

  const orderedEntries = useMemo(() => {
    if (layout === "column") return columnMajorOrder(visibleEntries, cols);
    return visibleEntries;
  }, [visibleEntries, cols, layout]);

  const gridClass = (() => {
    const border = showBorders ? "gap-px bg-white/[0.04]" : "gap-0";
    if (layout === "row") return `grid grid-flow-col auto-cols-fr ${border}`;
    if (layout === "column") return `grid ${border}`;
    return `grid ${border}`;
  })();

  const gridStyle = (() => {
    if (layout === "row") return undefined;
    return { gridTemplateColumns: `repeat(${cols}, 1fr)` };
  })();

  return (
    <div>
      <div className="flex justify-end items-center gap-3 px-4 lg:pr-0 mb-1">
        {layout === "grid" || layout === "column" ? (
          <label className="flex items-center gap-1 cursor-pointer">
            <span className="text-[9px] font-mono text-white/20">cols</span>
            <select
              value={cols}
              onChange={(e) => setCols(Number(e.target.value))}
              className="text-[9px] font-mono text-white/40 bg-transparent border-none outline-none cursor-pointer"
            >
              {COL_OPTIONS.map((n) => (
                <option key={n} value={n} className="bg-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]">
                  {n}
                </option>
              ))}
            </select>
          </label>
        ) : null}
        <label className="flex items-center gap-1 cursor-pointer">
          <span className="text-[9px] font-mono text-white/20">swatches</span>
          <select
            value={swatchCount}
            onChange={(e) => setSwatchCount(Number(e.target.value))}
            className="text-[9px] font-mono text-white/40 bg-transparent border-none outline-none cursor-pointer"
          >
            {STEP_OPTIONS.map((n) => (
              <option key={n} value={n} className="bg-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]">
                {n}
              </option>
            ))}
          </select>
        </label>
        <label className="flex items-center gap-1 cursor-pointer">
          <span className="text-[9px] font-mono text-white/20">layout</span>
          <select
            value={layout}
            onChange={(e) => setLayout(e.target.value as Layout)}
            className="text-[9px] font-mono text-white/40 bg-transparent border-none outline-none cursor-pointer"
          >
            <option value="grid" className="bg-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]">grid</option>
            <option value="column" className="bg-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]">column</option>
            <option value="row" className="bg-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]">row</option>
          </select>
        </label>
        <label className="flex items-center gap-1 cursor-pointer">
          <span className="text-[9px] font-mono text-white/20">labels</span>
          <button
            type="button"
            role="switch"
            aria-checked={showLabels}
            onClick={() => setShowLabels((v) => !v)}
            className="relative w-5 h-2.5 rounded-full transition-colors"
            style={{ backgroundColor: showLabels ? "rgba(255,255,255,0.25)" : "rgba(255,255,255,0.06)" }}
          >
            <span
              className="absolute top-px left-px w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-white/70 transition-transform"
              style={{ transform: showLabels ? "translateX(10px)" : "translateX(0)" }}
            />
          </button>
        </label>
        <label className="flex items-center gap-1 cursor-pointer">
          <span className="text-[9px] font-mono text-white/20">borders</span>
          <button
            type="button"
            role="switch"
            aria-checked={showBorders}
            onClick={() => setShowBorders((v) => !v)}
            className="relative w-5 h-2.5 rounded-full transition-colors"
            style={{ backgroundColor: showBorders ? "rgba(255,255,255,0.25)" : "rgba(255,255,255,0.06)" }}
          >
            <span
              className="absolute top-px left-px w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-white/70 transition-transform"
              style={{ transform: showBorders ? "translateX(10px)" : "translateX(0)" }}
            />
          </button>
        </label>
      </div>
      <div
        className={`ml-4 mr-4 lg:mr-0 rounded-xl overflow-hidden ${gridClass}`}
        style={gridStyle}
      >
        {orderedEntries.map((entry) => (
          <ColorSwatch
            key={entry.label}
            entry={entry}
            isCopied={copiedLabel === entry.label}
            onCopy={copy}
            showLabels={showLabels}
          />
        ))}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-13 13:22:51Z)**_

You're right, arbitrary columns is the wrong control. The meaningful orientations for this data are:

- **L → x**: lightness increases left→right, saturation varies on y (like a color picker)
- **L → y**: lightness increases top→bottom, saturation varies on x (rotated)

I'll auto-compute columns/rows from the swatch count to keep the grid full (no orphans) and as square as possible.

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
import { useMemo, useState } from "react";
import { ColorSwatch } from "./ColorSwatch";
import { useCopyToClipboard } from "../../hooks/useCopyToClipboard";
import type { ColorEntry } from "../../types/palette";

interface PaletteGridProps {
  entries: ColorEntry[];
}

const STEP_OPTIONS = [8, 10, 12, 16, 20, 24, 48, 98];

type Orientation = "L→x" | "L→y";

function sampleEntries(entries: ColorEntry[], count: number): ColorEntry[] {
  if (count >= entries.length) return entries;
  const step = (entries.length - 1) / (count - 1);
  return Array.from({ length: count }, (_, i) =>
    entries[Math.round(i * step)],
  );
}

// Find the column count that produces no orphans and the most square-ish grid
function bestCols(total: number): number {
  const target = Math.round(Math.sqrt(total));
  // Search outward from the square root for a clean divisor
  for (let d = 0; d <= target; d++) {
    if ((target + d) > 0 && total % (target + d) === 0) return target + d;
    if ((target - d) > 0 && total % (target - d) === 0) return target - d;
  }
  return target;
}

// Reorder row-major entries into column-major order for CSS grid rendering
function toColumnMajor(entries: ColorEntry[], cols: number): ColorEntry[] {
  const rows = Math.ceil(entries.length / cols);
  const reordered: ColorEntry[] = [];
  for (let r = 0; r < rows; r++) {
    for (let c = 0; c < cols; c++) {
      const idx = c * rows + r;
      if (idx < entries.length) reordered.push(entries[idx]);
    }
  }
  return reordered;
}

export function PaletteGrid({ entries }: PaletteGridProps) {
  const { copiedLabel, copy } = useCopyToClipboard();
  const [showBorders, setShowBorders] = useState(true);
  const [showLabels, setShowLabels] = useState(true);
  const [swatchCount, setSwatchCount] = useState<number>(98);
  const [orientation, setOrientation] = useState<Orientation>("L→x");

  const { displayEntries, cols } = useMemo(() => {
    const sampled = sampleEntries(entries, swatchCount);
    const cols = bestCols(sampled.length);

    if (orientation === "L→y") {
      return { displayEntries: toColumnMajor(sampled, cols), cols };
    }
    return { displayEntries: sampled, cols };
  }, [entries, swatchCount, orientation]);

  return (
    <div>
      <div className="flex justify-end items-center gap-3 px-4 lg:pr-0 mb-1">
        <label className="flex items-center gap-1 cursor-pointer">
          <span className="text-[9px] font-mono text-white/20">swatches</span>
          <select
            value={swatchCount}
            onChange={(e) => setSwatchCount(Number(e.target.value))}
            className="text-[9px] font-mono text-white/40 bg-transparent border-none outline-none cursor-pointer"
          >
            {STEP_OPTIONS.map((n) => (
              <option key={n} value={n} className="bg-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]">
                {n}
              </option>
            ))}
          </select>
        </label>
        <label className="flex items-center gap-1 cursor-pointer">
          <span className="text-[9px] font-mono text-white/20">orientation</span>
          <select
            value={orientation}
            onChange={(e) => setOrientation(e.target.value as Orientation)}
            className="text-[9px] font-mono text-white/40 bg-transparent border-none outline-none cursor-pointer"
          >
            <option value="L→x" className="bg-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]">L → x</option>
            <option value="L→y" className="bg-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]">L → y</option>
          </select>
        </label>
        <label className="flex items-center gap-1 cursor-pointer">
          <span className="text-[9px] font-mono text-white/20">labels</span>
          <button
            type="button"
            role="switch"
            aria-checked={showLabels}
            onClick={() => setShowLabels((v) => !v)}
            className="relative w-5 h-2.5 rounded-full transition-colors"
            style={{ backgroundColor: showLabels ? "rgba(255,255,255,0.25)" : "rgba(255,255,255,0.06)" }}
          >
            <span
              className="absolute top-px left-px w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-white/70 transition-transform"
              style={{ transform: showLabels ? "translateX(10px)" : "translateX(0)" }}
            />
          </button>
        </label>
        <label className="flex items-center gap-1 cursor-pointer">
          <span className="text-[9px] font-mono text-white/20">borders</span>
          <button
            type="button"
            role="switch"
            aria-checked={showBorders}
            onClick={() => setShowBorders((v) => !v)}
            className="relative w-5 h-2.5 rounded-full transition-colors"
            style={{ backgroundColor: showBorders ? "rgba(255,255,255,0.25)" : "rgba(255,255,255,0.06)" }}
          >
            <span
              className="absolute top-px left-px w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-white/70 transition-transform"
              style={{ transform: showBorders ? "translateX(10px)" : "translateX(0)" }}
            />
          </button>
        </label>
      </div>
      <div
        className={`grid ml-4 mr-4 lg:mr-0 rounded-xl overflow-hidden ${showBorders ? "gap-px bg-white/[0.04]" : "gap-0"}`}
        style={{ gridTemplateColumns: `repeat(${cols}, 1fr)` }}
      >
        {displayEntries.map((entry) => (
          <ColorSwatch
            key={entry.label}
            entry={entry}
            isCopied={copiedLabel === entry.label}
            onCopy={copy}
            showLabels={showLabels}
          />
        ))}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

```

```tsx
import { getContrastColor } from "../../lib/colors";
import { CopyFeedback } from "./CopyFeedback";
import type { ColorEntry } from "../../types/palette";

interface ColorSwatchProps {
  entry: ColorEntry;
  isCopied: boolean;
  onCopy: (text: string, label: string) => void;
  showLabels?: boolean;
  compact?: boolean;
}

export function ColorSwatch({ entry, isCopied, onCopy, showLabels = true, compact = false }: ColorSwatchProps) {
  const textColor = getContrastColor(entry.hex);

  return (
    <button
      type="button"
      className={`relative flex flex-col justify-center gap-0.5 text-left cursor-pointer focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-white/50 focus-visible:ring-offset-1 focus-visible:ring-offset-[hsl(0_0%_6%)] group ${compact ? "px-1.5 py-1 min-h-[40px]" : "px-4 py-3 min-h-[72px]"}`}
      style={{ backgroundColor: entry.hex, color: textColor }}
      onClick={() => onCopy(`hsl(${entry.hslString})`, entry.label)}
      title={`Copy ${entry.label}`}
    >
      {showLabels && (
        <>
          <span className={`font-semibold transition-transform group-hover:scale-105 origin-left ${compact ? "text-[9px]" : "text-[13px]"}`}>{entry.label}</span>
          <span className={`font-mono opacity-75 transition-transform group-hover:scale-105 origin-left ${compact ? "text-[7px]" : "text-[11px]"}`}>
            hsl({entry.hslString})
          </span>
        </>
      )}
      <CopyFeedback visible={isCopied} />
    </button>
  );
}

```

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user is iteratively refining a neutral color palette generator app (React/Vite/TypeScript). This session focused on: making the reference hue adjustable via a popover, converting the UI chrome from amber accent + blue-tinted greys to pure achromatic grayscale, reorganizing UI text, making the adjustment slider linear, adding visualization graphs, and adding grid display controls (swatch count, orientation, borders, labels toggles). The overarching goal is a precision color tool where the only saturated colors are the computed palette itself.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Parabolic saturation curve: `getSaturation(lightness, sMod)` with `sMod=0` meaning no adjustment
   - React/Vite/TypeScript, Tailwind v4, Radix UI (Dialog, Popover, Slider, Tabs), Framer Motion, chroma-js, Zustand
   - Pure grayscale UI chrome (`hsl(0 0% X%)`) — no saturated colors in UI elements
   - Linear slider mapping: display percentage → internal sMod via `2500 / -pct`
   - Column-major grid reordering for "rotated" orientation
   - Orphan prevention via `bestCols()` — finds nearest divisor to square root
   - Compact swatch mode for high density grids
   - Small toggle switches (9px label, 20px track) for unobtrusive controls
   - Native `<details>` for comparison strip twirldown

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`app/src/components/About.tsx`**
     - Central modal component. Hue picker refactored from inline toggle to Radix Popover anchored to swatch. All `hsl(220 10% X%)` replaced with `hsl(0 0% X%)`. All `amber` references replaced with white. `AboutCaption` export removed. `HuePicker` component with preset swatches, reset icon, gradient bar. All visualization components accept `hue` prop.
     - Key constants: `DEFAULT_HUE = 220`, `REF_SMOD = 40`, `STRIP_STEPS = 9`
     - HuePicker with presets above gradient bar:
     ```tsx
     const HUE_PRESETS: { label: string; hue: number }[] = [
       { label: "Red", hue: 0 }, { label: "Orange", hue: 30 },
       { label: "Gold", hue: 45 }, { label: "Green", hue: 150 },
       { label: "Teal", hue: 180 }, { label: "Blue", hue: 220 },
       { label: "Indigo", hue: 245 }, { label: "Purple", hue: 280 },
       { label: "Rose", hue: 340 },
     ];
     ```
     - Popover wrapping the swatch:
     ```tsx
     <Popover.Root>
       <Popover.Trigger asChild>
         <button className="h-full w-full rounded-sm cursor-pointer hover:ring-1 hover:ring-white/20 transition-shadow" style={{ backgroundColor: `hsl(${hue} 100% 50%)` }} />
       </Popover.Trigger>
       <Popover.Portal>
         <Popover.Content side="right" sideOffset={12} align="center" className="w-56 p-3 rounded-lg bg-[hsl(0_0%_10%)] border border-white/[0.08] shadow-xl z-[60]">
           <HuePicker hue={hue} onChange={setHue} onReset={() => setHue(DEFAULT_HUE)} isDefault={hue === DEFAULT_HUE} />
           <Popover.Arrow className="fill-[hsl(0_0%_10%)]" />
         </Popover.Content>
       </Popover.Portal>
     </Popover.Root>
     ```

   - **`app/src/lib/palette.ts`**
     - Core algorithm. Added `sMod === 0` guard returning 1 (flat multiplier). Added duplicate entry prevention via `Set<number>`.
     ```typescript
     export function getSaturation(lightness: number, sMod: number): number {
       if (sMod === 0) return 1;
       const o = 50;
       return 1 + (Math.pow(lightness - o, 2) / sMod - Math.pow(o, 2) / sMod) / 100;
     }
     ```
     - Duplicate fix in generateNeutrals:
     ```typescript
     const seen = new Set<number>();
     // ... inside loop:
     if (!seen.has(i)) { seen.add(i); /* push entry */ }
     ```

   - **`app/src/components/controls/AdjustmentSlider.tsx`**
     - Refactored from linear-in-sMod to linear-in-percentage. Range -100 to 0 (0% = no curve).
     ```typescript
     function pctToSMod(pct: number): number {
       if (pct === 0) return 0;
       return 2500 / -pct;
     }
     function sModToPct(sMod: number): number {
       if (sMod === 0) return 0;
       return Math.round(-2500 / sMod);
     }
     ```

   - **`app/src/components/curves/SaturationCurve.tsx`**
     - Renamed title to "Saturation Adjustment". Made square (400x400). Larger grid labels (13px). Round y-axis values (0.25 or 0.5 steps, always including 1.0). Added comparison strips in `<details>` twirldown. Added steps toggle. Removed keys/helper text.
     ```typescript
     const W = 400;
     const H = 400;
     ```
     - Y-axis grid with round values:
     ```typescript
     const step = yMax - yMin > 1.5 ? 0.5 : 0.25;
     // ... always includes 1.0
     ```
     - Comparison strips (unadjusted vs adjusted) in twirldown
     - Small toggle switch for palette steps

   - **`app/src/components/curves/EffectiveSaturation.tsx`**
     - New file. Shows final computed saturation (saturation × multiplier) vs lightness. Fixed 0-100% y-axis. Dashed baseline at base saturation. Same toggle pattern.
     ```typescript
     const yMin = 0;
     const yMax = 1; // fixed 0–100% range
     ```

   - **`app/src/components/palette/PaletteGrid.tsx`**
     - Major rewrite. Added: swatch count selector (8-98), orientation (picker/rotated), labels toggle, borders toggle. Auto-computes columns via `bestCols()`. Trims orphans. Column-major reordering for rotated view.
     ```typescript
     const STEP_OPTIONS = [8, 10, 12, 16, 20, 24, 48, 98];
     type Orientation = "picker" | "rotated";

     function bestCols(total: number): number {
       const target = Math.round(Math.sqrt(total));
       for (let d = 0; d <= target; d++) {
         if ((target + d) > 0 && total % (target + d) === 0) return target + d;
         if ((target - d) > 0 && total % (target - d) === 0) return target - d;
       }
       return target;
     }

     function toColumnMajor(entries: ColorEntry[], cols: number): ColorEntry[] {
       const rows = Math.ceil(entries.length / cols);
       const reordered: ColorEntry[] = [];
       for (let r = 0; r < rows; r++) {
         for (let c = 0; c < cols; c++) {
           const idx = c * rows + r;
           if (idx < entries.length) reordered.push(entries[idx]);
         }
       }
       return reordered;
     }
     ```
     - Orientation logic:
     ```typescript
     if (orientation === "picker") {
       // Light at top, dark at bottom — row-major
       return { displayEntries: sampled, cols };
     }
     // Rotated: dark at top-left, light at bottom-right — column-major
     const reversed = sampled.slice().reverse();
     return { displayEntries: toColumnMajor(reversed, cols), cols };
     ```

   - **`app/src/components/palette/ColorSwatch.tsx`**
     - Added `showLabels` and `compact` props. Hover scales only text (not container). Focus ring uses white instead of amber.
     ```typescript
     interface ColorSwatchProps {
       entry: ColorEntry;
       isCopied: boolean;
       onCopy: (text: string, label: string) => void;
       showLabels?: boolean;
       compact?: boolean;
     }
     ```
     - Compact mode: `px-1.5 py-1 min-h-[40px]`, text `9px`/`7px` vs normal `13px`/`11px`

   - **`app/src/components/palette/PaletteStrip.tsx`**
     - Reversed entries so strip goes dark (left) to light (right): `{[...entries].reverse().map(...)}`

   - **`app/src/components/Header.tsx`**
     - Updated subtitle to "Tinted neutral palettes with parabolic saturation correction". Added "Click swatch to copy" hint next to Export button.

   - **`app/src/App.tsx`**

     ```typescript
     export function getSaturation(lightness: number, sMod: number): number {

     }
     ```

     ```typescript
     const STEP_OPTIONS = [8, 10, 12, 16, 20, 24, 48, 98];
     type Orientation = "picker" | "rotated";

     function bestCols(total: number): number {
       const target = Math.round(Math.sqrt(total));
       for (let d = 0; d <= target; d++) {
         if ((target + d) > 0 && total % (target + d) === 0) return target + d;
         if ((target - d) > 0 && total % (target - d) === 0) return target - d;
       }
       return target;
     }

     function toColumnMajor(entries: ColorEntry[], cols: number): ColorEntry[] {
       const rows = Math.ceil(entries.length / cols);
       const reordered: ColorEntry[] = [];
       for (let r = 0; r < rows; r++) {
         for (let c = 0; c < cols; c++) {
           const idx = c * rows + r;
           if (idx < entries.length) reordered.push(entries[idx]);
         }
       }
       return reordered;
     }
     ```
     - Orientation logic:
     ```typescript
     if (orientation === "picker") {
       // Light at top, dark at bottom — row-major
       return { displayEntries: sampled, cols };
     }
     // Rotated: dark at top-left, light at bottom-right — column-major
     const reversed = sampled.slice().reverse();
     return { displayEntries: toColumnMajor(reversed, cols), cols };
     ```
